verses.text: David sets apart the sons of Asaph, Heman, and Jeduthun for the temple's music, 'to prophesy with harps, with psalteries, and with cymbals.' Sung praise is called prophecy. To lead God's people in worship is a holy, Spirit-touched calling, not mere performance.

David sets apart the sons of Asaph, Heman, and Jeduthun for the temple's music, 'to prophesy with harps, with psalteries, and with cymbals.' Sung praise is called prophecy. To lead God's people in worship is a holy, Spirit-touched calling, not mere performance.

David sets apart the sons of Asaph, Heman, and Jeduthun for the temple's music, 'to prophesy with harps, with psalteries, and with cymbals.' Sung praise is called prophecy. To lead God's people in worship is a holy, Spirit-touched calling, not mere performance.

Heman's many sons and the trained singers — 288 in all, 'cunning in the songs of the LORD' — are numbered. Skill is sought for sacred music. God deserves trained gifts and diligent preparation in the praise His people offer Him.

Heman's many sons and the trained singers — 288 in all, 'cunning in the songs of the LORD' — are numbered. Skill is sought for sacred music. God deserves trained gifts and diligent preparation in the praise His people offer Him.

The singers are divided by lot into courses, 'as well the small as the great, the teacher as the scholar.' The lot makes no distinction of rank. In God's service the master and the pupil stand equal, each given his place by God.

The singers are divided by lot into courses, 'as well the small as the great, the teacher as the scholar.' The lot makes no distinction of rank. In God's service the master and the pupil stand equal, each given his place by God.

More courses fall by lot, each of twelve singers with their sons and brethren. The praise of God is ordered in steady rotation. Worship is not left to impulse but arranged so the song continues, course after course.

More courses fall by lot, each of twelve singers with their sons and brethren. The praise of God is ordered in steady rotation. Worship is not left to impulse but arranged so the song continues, course after course.
The lots continue, naming course after course of appointed singers. Every family takes its turn. God's house is filled with continual praise because each is content to serve in his assigned season.

Still the lots are cast, the roll of singers running on. The careful record honours each appointed group. No servant of God's praise is left unnamed or without his place.

Still the lots are cast, the roll of singers running on. The careful record honours each appointed group. No servant of God's praise is left unnamed or without his place.

The last courses of singers are named, completing the twenty-four. The whole ministry of music is set in order. What David arranged would fill the temple with praise for generations, each course faithful in its turn.
